records.title: Choose and compare an energy supplier
records.description: Households can compare electricity and gas offers by contract type, duration, total expected cost, sustainability claims, cancellation terms and the supplier's current ACM licence.
records.ai_summary: Compare offers using the same annual consumption and include every fixed, variable and conditional component. Verify the licence and read the duration, price-change and cancellation clauses before accepting.

records.title: Move an energy contract to a new home
records.description: When moving, a household can often transfer its current energy contract or arrange another supplier, while reporting both addresses and preserving final and initial meter readings.
records.ai_summary: Notify the supplier before the move, photograph meter readings at both homes and provide them on time if the meters do not transmit automatically. Keep the final bill and check the recorded readings.

records.title: End or switch an energy contract
records.description: The right cost of ending an energy contract depends on whether it has fixed, variable or dynamic terms, its remaining duration and the cancellation calculation disclosed by the supplier.

official_source.title: Energiecontract opzeggen: alles over opzegvergoeding en opzegtermijn

records.ai_summary: Ask the current supplier for the expected cancellation charge before switching and compare it with the savings. Retain the contract, quote, notice confirmation and final account if disputing a fee.

records.title: Verify an energy supplier's licence
records.description: Suppliers serving household electricity and gas customers require an ACM licence and must meet requirements for reliable supply, clear offers, reasonable payment and complaint handling.
records.ai_summary: Confirm the legal supplier named in the contract and check that it appears on the current ACM list. A reseller may sell the offer, but the licensed supplier remains responsible for delivery.

records.title: Understand electricity and gas grid costs
records.description: Regional grid operators own and maintain local connections and networks. Households cannot choose the regional operator, while ACM sets maximum regulated components of grid charges.

official_source.title: Netbeheerkosten: capaciteitstarief, vastrecht, aansluittarief en meettarief

records.ai_summary: Separate supplier charges from regulated grid costs when reading the bill. The regional operator is fixed by address, and the charge depends partly on connection capacity and current regulated tariffs.

records.title: Report a gas smell or power emergency
records.description: A gas smell, loss of gas or electricity, or meter fault can be reported through the national utility emergency number, after taking immediate safety measures and leaving danger to professionals.
records.ai_summary: For a gas smell, close the main valve if safe, ventilate without operating electrical switches, avoid flames, call the national emergency number and wait outside for instructions.

attributes.emergency_contact: National gas and electricity fault number

records.title: Compensation after a power outage
records.description: A household may qualify for regulated compensation when an unplanned electricity interruption lasts beyond the applicable threshold, depending on connection size, duration and the cause recorded by the operator.
records.ai_summary: Make sure the interruption is known to the grid operator and record its timing. The official page explains current thresholds, exclusions, payment timing and the complaint route if compensation is missing.

records.title: Consumer rights for district heating
records.description: Small district-heating and block-heating users may have statutory protection covering the supply agreement, maximum tariffs, metering, billing, interruptions, disconnection and complaint handling.

official_source.title: Uw rechten bij warmte via een warmteleverancier

records.ai_summary: Identify whether the household contracts directly with a heat supplier or pays through a landlord or owners' association, because the applicable protection and dispute route can differ.

records.title: Drinking-water charges and tax
records.description: Household water bills combine drinking-water supply components with the national tax on mains water, whose scope and thresholds can change and should be checked for the relevant billing year.
records.ai_summary: Check the local water supplier's bill and the current government tax rules for the billing year. Drinking-water supply, municipal sewer charges and water-authority treatment charges are separate items.

attributes.responsible_authority: Water supplier and Dutch tax system

records.title: Sewer and wastewater-treatment charges
records.description: Wastewater is commonly funded through a municipal sewer charge and a separate water-authority treatment charge, with responsibility for questions depending on which levy is disputed.

official_source.title: Hoe wordt de riolering en afvalwaterzuivering betaald?

records.ai_summary: Read the assessment to distinguish the municipality's sewer levy from the water authority's treatment levy. Contact the issuing body for the calculation, objection deadline and any available remission process.
